sectionName: Biological & Biochemical Foundations of Living Systems

questions.passage: Researchers investigated human fibroblasts carrying knockdowns of nucleotide-excision repair or mismatch-repair proteins because different DNA lesions are repaired by different pathways, and confusing lesion type with repair pathway can lead to incorrect interpretation of survival assays. The investigators framed the project around a narrow mechanistic question: whether UV sensitivity and replication-error accumulation came from the same repair defect or from separable pathway disruptions. Rather than treating the phenotype as a single endpoint, they collected biochemical, cellular, and control measurements so that an apparent association could be separated from a direct pathway effect. The primary experiment compared control cells, XPA knockdown cells, MLH1 knockdown cells, and double-knockdown cells. Samples were prepared under matched conditions for cell number, total protein, incubation time, and buffer composition. The main assay used clonogenic survival after UV exposure plus a microsatellite instability reporter after several cell divisions. Figure 1 reports XPA knockdown sharply reduced UV survival, MLH1 knockdown increased microsatellite instability, and the double knockdown combined both phenotypes. Values are means from independent preparations, and the error bars in the original analysis were small enough that the ordering of the groups was reproducible across trials. The authors did not use the figure alone as proof of mechanism; instead, they used it to choose which follow-up experiment would best distinguish between competing explanations. Several controls were built into the design. Knockdown efficiency was confirmed by immunoblotting, cell-cycle distribution before treatment was similar, and transfection with scrambled siRNA did not change either endpoint. These details matter because many superficially attractive interpretations would predict a global change in viability, loading, or nonspecific assay performance rather than a pathway-specific change. The investigators also measured unscheduled DNA synthesis after UV damage, which allowed them to ask whether the perturbation changed expression, localization, catalytic activity, or signal propagation. A follow-up experiment tested whether reintroducing siRNA-resistant XPA restored UV survival without correcting microsatellite instability. This experiment was deliberately designed to avoid a common interpretive trap: a DNA repair protein can affect one class of lesion without explaining every genome-instability phenotype. They avoided claiming that every downstream physiological effect had been proven, because the study did not include whole-animal outcomes or long-term compensation. The figure provides quantitative evidence about the immediate assay readout, the controls determine whether that readout is technically interpretable, and the follow-up experiment tests whether the proposed mechanism survives an additional perturbation.
questions.stem: Which conclusion is best supported by Figure 1?

choices.A: MLH1 knockdown is the primary cause of UV hypersensitivity in this experiment.
choices.B: XPA knockdown prevents all DNA replication, making the microsatellite reporter uninterpretable.
choices.C: XPA and MLH1 knockdowns produce identical phenotypes for both UV survival and microsatellite instability, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.D: XPA loss mainly compromises repair of UV-type lesions, whereas MLH1 loss mainly compromises correction of replication-associated mismatches.

explanations.A: MLH1 knockdown has relatively preserved UV survival compared with XPA knockdown. Here, the relevant evidence is that XPA knockdown primarily reduces UV survival, whereas MLH1 knockdown primarily elevates the microsatellite reporter. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.B: XPA knockdown does not strongly elevate the MSI reporter and baseline cell-cycle distribution was similar. Here, the relevant evidence is that XPA knockdown primarily reduces UV survival, whereas MLH1 knockdown primarily elevates the microsatellite reporter. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.C: The two single knockdowns separate strongly across the two endpoints. Here, the relevant evidence is that XPA knockdown primarily reduces UV survival, whereas MLH1 knockdown primarily elevates the microsatellite reporter. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.D: Correct. XPA knockdown primarily reduces UV survival, whereas MLH1 knockdown primarily elevates the microsatellite reporter. The relevant evidence is that XPA knockdown primarily reduces UV survival, whereas MLH1 knockdown primarily elevates the microsatellite reporter. This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. The figure shows pathway specificity across two lesion classes rather than a single global repair failure. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.

questions.passageImageCaption: Figure 1. UV survival and microsatellite instability reporter activity after repair-protein knockdown.
questions.passageHelp:
The researchers concluded that the data supported a specific model, but they noted that the passage evidence was strongest for the step directly measured by the assay.

In interpreting the study, the reader should keep the experimental hierarchy in mind.

Answers that merely name a molecule from the passage are usually weaker than answers that explain how the measured intervention changes the pathway while leaving alternative explanations less consistent with the observations.

A strong answer therefore has to account for the numerical trend, the biological pathway, and the limits of the design.

questions.stem: Why does reintroducing siRNA-resistant XPA test specificity?

choices.A: It permanently edits MLH1 into XPA, creating a hybrid repair enzyme.
choices.B: It proves that all UV lesions are repaired by mismatch repair.
choices.C: It removes the need for a scrambled siRNA control in the original experiment, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.D: It asks whether restoring the targeted NER protein rescues the UV phenotype without changing unrelated mismatch-repair loss.

explanations.A: Rescue expression does not convert one gene into another. Here, the relevant evidence is that XPA knockdown primarily reduces UV survival, whereas MLH1 knockdown primarily elevates the microsatellite reporter. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.B: XPA is part of nucleotide-excision repair, not mismatch repair. Here, the relevant evidence is that XPA knockdown primarily reduces UV survival, whereas MLH1 knockdown primarily elevates the microsatellite reporter. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.C: Rescue and scrambled controls address different concerns and both are useful. Here, the relevant evidence is that XPA knockdown primarily reduces UV survival, whereas MLH1 knockdown primarily elevates the microsatellite reporter. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.D: Correct. XPA rescue should restore the phenotype caused specifically by XPA depletion. The relevant evidence is that XPA knockdown primarily reduces UV survival, whereas MLH1 knockdown primarily elevates the microsatellite reporter. This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. An siRNA-resistant construct controls for off-target knockdown and helps localize the UV phenotype to the intended protein. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.

questions.stem: What is the role of the scrambled siRNA control?

choices.A: It creates a positive control for microsatellite instability by disabling MLH1.
choices.B: It controls for nonspecific effects of transfection and siRNA exposure on survival or reporter activity.
choices.C: It provides the lesion-specific repair protein missing from XPA knockdown cells, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.D: It measures unscheduled DNA synthesis directly after UV exposure.

explanations.A: Scrambled siRNA is designed not to target MLH1 or other specific repair genes. Here, the relevant evidence is that XPA knockdown primarily reduces UV survival, whereas MLH1 knockdown primarily elevates the microsatellite reporter. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.B: Correct. Scrambled siRNA controls for the procedure rather than a specific repair protein. The relevant evidence is that XPA knockdown primarily reduces UV survival, whereas MLH1 knockdown primarily elevates the microsatellite reporter. This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. Controls isolate the biological effect of knockdown from delivery-related stress. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.
explanations.C: A scrambled siRNA is not a rescue construct and does not encode XPA. Here, the relevant evidence is that XPA knockdown primarily reduces UV survival, whereas MLH1 knockdown primarily elevates the microsatellite reporter. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.D: UDS is a separate assay, not the purpose of the scrambled siRNA. Here, the relevant evidence is that XPA knockdown primarily reduces UV survival, whereas MLH1 knockdown primarily elevates the microsatellite reporter. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.

questions.stem: If unscheduled DNA synthesis after UV is low only in XPA knockdown cells, what would that indicate?

choices.A: The cells have completed homologous recombination at every stalled replication fork, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.B: The cells have impaired nucleotide-excision repair synthesis after bulky UV-induced damage.
choices.C: The cells have higher mitochondrial beta-oxidation during UV exposure.
choices.D: The cells have increased mismatch-repair correction of microsatellite loops.

explanations.A: UDS after UV does not prove complete homologous recombination. Here, the relevant evidence is that XPA knockdown primarily reduces UV survival, whereas MLH1 knockdown primarily elevates the microsatellite reporter. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.B: Correct. XPA functions in nucleotide-excision repair, which removes bulky helix-distorting lesions and fills the resulting gap. The relevant evidence is that XPA knockdown primarily reduces UV survival, whereas MLH1 knockdown primarily elevates the microsatellite reporter. This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. A pathway-specific biochemical readout strengthens the survival phenotype. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.
explanations.C: Mitochondrial metabolism is not the measured endpoint in this repair assay. Here, the relevant evidence is that XPA knockdown primarily reduces UV survival, whereas MLH1 knockdown primarily elevates the microsatellite reporter. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.D: UDS after UV is a nucleotide-excision repair readout, not a microsatellite mismatch readout. Here, the relevant evidence is that XPA knockdown primarily reduces UV survival, whereas MLH1 knockdown primarily elevates the microsatellite reporter. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.

questions.stem: Which claim would be too broad?

choices.A: Any cell with microsatellite instability must also be hypersensitive to UV because all repair defects are equivalent.
choices.B: MLH1 knockdown increases the microsatellite reporter.
choices.C: The double knockdown combines high MSI with poor UV survival, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.D: XPA knockdown reduces survival after UV exposure.

explanations.A: Correct. The two single knockdowns show that repair defects are not interchangeable. The relevant evidence is that XPA knockdown primarily reduces UV survival, whereas MLH1 knockdown primarily elevates the microsatellite reporter. This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. The appropriate inference distinguishes pathway-specific phenotypes rather than lumping all genome instability together. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.
explanations.B: That is directly shown by the reporter endpoint. Here, the relevant evidence is that XPA knockdown primarily reduces UV survival, whereas MLH1 knockdown primarily elevates the microsatellite reporter. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.C: The figure supports a combined phenotype in the double knockdown. Here, the relevant evidence is that XPA knockdown primarily reduces UV survival, whereas MLH1 knockdown primarily elevates the microsatellite reporter. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.D: The UV-survival bar is much lower for XPA knockdown. Here, the relevant evidence is that XPA knockdown primarily reduces UV survival, whereas MLH1 knockdown primarily elevates the microsatellite reporter. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.

questions.passage: Researchers investigated intestinal epithelial cells transitioning from fetal to adult lactase-expression states because developmental gene expression often changes through promoter methylation, enhancer accessibility, and transcription-factor occupancy rather than through changes in coding sequence. The investigators framed the project around a narrow mechanistic question: whether reduced lactase expression reflected promoter methylation, loss of an enhancer-binding factor, or accelerated mRNA degradation. Rather than treating the phenotype as a single endpoint, they collected biochemical, cellular, and control measurements so that an apparent association could be separated from a direct pathway effect. The primary experiment compared cells with high lactase expression, cells after differentiation, differentiated cells treated with a DNA methyltransferase inhibitor, and cells overexpressing an enhancer-binding transcription factor. Samples were prepared under matched conditions for cell number, total protein, incubation time, and buffer composition. The main assay used RT-qPCR, bisulfite sequencing, and chromatin accessibility measurements at the lactase regulatory region. Figure 1 reports differentiation increased promoter methylation and reduced mRNA; methyltransferase inhibition partially restored mRNA and accessibility, while transcription-factor overexpression was strongest when methylation was low. Values are means from independent preparations, and the error bars in the original analysis were small enough that the ordering of the groups was reproducible across trials. The authors did not use the figure alone as proof of mechanism; instead, they used it to choose which follow-up experiment would best distinguish between competing explanations. Several controls were built into the design. A housekeeping transcript, global RNA integrity, and a nearby unrelated promoter were unchanged. These details matter because many superficially attractive interpretations would predict a global change in viability, loading, or nonspecific assay performance rather than a pathway-specific change. The investigators also measured nascent transcription using intronic qPCR primers, which allowed them to ask whether the perturbation changed expression, localization, catalytic activity, or signal propagation. A follow-up experiment tested whether demethylation restored transcription initiation rather than merely stabilizing mature mRNA. This experiment was deliberately designed to avoid a common interpretive trap: higher mature mRNA can result from more transcription, slower degradation, or changes in cell composition. They avoided claiming that every downstream physiological effect had been proven, because the study did not include whole-animal outcomes or long-term compensation. The figure provides quantitative evidence about the immediate assay readout, the controls determine whether that readout is technically interpretable, and the follow-up experiment tests whether the proposed mechanism survives an additional perturbation.
questions.stem: Which interpretation best fits the regulatory data?

choices.A: Differentiation increases lactase mRNA by methylating the promoter.
choices.B: The coding sequence of lactase is deleted during normal differentiation.
choices.C: Promoter methylation is associated with reduced lactase transcription, and lowering methylation partially restores expression.
choices.D: Transcription-factor overexpression completely bypasses methylation in every condition, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.

explanations.A: The differentiated state has high methylation and low mRNA. Here, the relevant evidence is that Differentiated cells show high promoter methylation and low mRNA; DNMT inhibition moves both measures toward the high-expression state. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.B: The experiment measures regulatory marks, not a deletion of the coding region. Here, the relevant evidence is that Differentiated cells show high promoter methylation and low mRNA; DNMT inhibition moves both measures toward the high-expression state. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.C: Correct. Differentiated cells show high promoter methylation and low mRNA; DNMT inhibition moves both measures toward the high-expression state. The relevant evidence is that Differentiated cells show high promoter methylation and low mRNA; DNMT inhibition moves both measures toward the high-expression state. This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. The figure supports an inverse relationship between promoter methylation and expression. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.
explanations.D: Overexpression is less effective when methylation remains high. Here, the relevant evidence is that Differentiated cells show high promoter methylation and low mRNA; DNMT inhibition moves both measures toward the high-expression state. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.

The researchers concluded that the data supported a specific model, but they noted that the passage evidence was strongest for the step directly measured by the assay.

In interpreting the study, the reader should keep the experimental hierarchy in mind.

Answers that merely name a molecule from the passage are usually weaker than answers that explain how the measured intervention changes the pathway while leaving alternative explanations less consistent with the observations.

A strong answer therefore has to account for the numerical trend, the biological pathway, and the limits of the design.

questions.stem: Why are intronic qPCR primers useful in the follow-up experiment?

choices.A: They enrich for newly synthesized pre-mRNA and help distinguish transcription initiation from mature mRNA stabilization.
choices.B: They prove that the enhancer-binding factor changes the lactase amino acid sequence, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.C: They bind only to the lactase protein after translation is complete.
choices.D: They measure DNA methylation directly by cutting methylated cytosines.

explanations.A: Correct. Nascent transcript measurements help determine whether the treatment increases transcription rather than simply slowing degradation. The relevant evidence is that Differentiated cells show high promoter methylation and low mRNA; DNMT inhibition moves both measures toward the high-expression state. This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. The passage specifically raises mature mRNA stability as an alternative explanation. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.
explanations.B: Intronic primers do not measure coding changes or protein sequence. Here, the relevant evidence is that Differentiated cells show high promoter methylation and low mRNA; DNMT inhibition moves both measures toward the high-expression state. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.C: qPCR primers bind nucleic acids, not translated protein. Here, the relevant evidence is that Differentiated cells show high promoter methylation and low mRNA; DNMT inhibition moves both measures toward the high-expression state. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.D: Bisulfite sequencing, not intronic qPCR, measures methylation. Here, the relevant evidence is that Differentiated cells show high promoter methylation and low mRNA; DNMT inhibition moves both measures toward the high-expression state. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.

questions.stem: Why was a nearby unrelated promoter measured?

choices.A: It serves as the substrate-binding site for lactase enzyme activity.
choices.B: It converts fetal epithelial cells into pancreatic beta cells.
choices.C: It tests whether methylation changes are localized to the lactase regulatory region rather than globally disrupting all promoters.
choices.D: It guarantees that all enhancer sites in the genome are unmethylated, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.

explanations.A: A promoter is a DNA regulatory region, not the enzyme substrate-binding site. Here, the relevant evidence is that Differentiated cells show high promoter methylation and low mRNA; DNMT inhibition moves both measures toward the high-expression state. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.B: The promoter control does not define cell lineage conversion. Here, the relevant evidence is that Differentiated cells show high promoter methylation and low mRNA; DNMT inhibition moves both measures toward the high-expression state. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.C: Correct. The unrelated promoter control supports specificity of the regulatory effect. The relevant evidence is that Differentiated cells show high promoter methylation and low mRNA; DNMT inhibition moves both measures toward the high-expression state. This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. Epigenetic drugs can have broad effects, so locus-specific controls are important. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.
explanations.D: A nearby control cannot prove genome-wide enhancer status. Here, the relevant evidence is that Differentiated cells show high promoter methylation and low mRNA; DNMT inhibition moves both measures toward the high-expression state. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.

questions.stem: If DNMT inhibition restores nascent lactase transcripts before mature mRNA rises, what does that support?

choices.A: The lactase enzyme has become a DNA methyltransferase.
choices.B: Promoter methylation is irrelevant because nascent transcript levels changed first, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.C: Demethylation increases transcription initiation upstream of the later increase in mature mRNA.
choices.D: Mature mRNA is being translated into DNA before transcription changes.

explanations.A: The treatment targets methylation machinery; it does not convert lactase into an enzyme that methylates DNA. Here, the relevant evidence is that Differentiated cells show high promoter methylation and low mRNA; DNMT inhibition moves both measures toward the high-expression state. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.B: A change in nascent transcription after demethylation supports, rather than refutes, promoter regulation. Here, the relevant evidence is that Differentiated cells show high promoter methylation and low mRNA; DNMT inhibition moves both measures toward the high-expression state. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.C: Correct. A temporal increase in pre-mRNA indicates that transcriptional output changes before mature mRNA accumulation. The relevant evidence is that Differentiated cells show high promoter methylation and low mRNA; DNMT inhibition moves both measures toward the high-expression state. This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. Time-course logic can distinguish cause from downstream accumulation. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.
explanations.D: RNA is not translated into DNA in this context. Here, the relevant evidence is that Differentiated cells show high promoter methylation and low mRNA; DNMT inhibition moves both measures toward the high-expression state. The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.

choices.A: The passage supports regulatory control of lactase expression in these cells but does not prove lifelong lactose tolerance in a person.
choices.B: Promoter methylation is higher in the differentiated low-expression condition.
